response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack engineer with strong web/backend credentials, but the role explicitly requires 8 years of C++ programming and 5 years of large-scale infrastructure/distributed-systems experience as minimum qualifications. The resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments (~6 months in 2014–2015) and no production cloud compute platform leadership. Primary expertise is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, which does not substitute for the stated C++ depth or technical-leadership scope on internal compute platforms.
strengths: Long track record across backend, web, mobile and embedded systems with senior/staff titles
Distributed/high-throughput exposure at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP req/s) using async I/O and high-performance databases
Demonstrated mentoring, workshops, and open-source leadership (GISCollective, Trial, Fluent-Asserts)
concerns: Falls far short of the 8-year C++ minimum — only short NI LabWindows/CVI C/C++ work
No evidence of leading large-scale cloud compute platform architecture or production cloud infrastructure ownership
No formal technical leadership role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers at the scale implied by the JD

key_factors.factor: Lack of cloud compute platform leadership
